situation: AFL plateaued fuzzing the IBM TPM 2.0 command server. Inputs are sequences of TPM commands. Coverage stalls: single commands are reached, but deep states need specific ORDERED command sequences (the command-to-command transitions) that blind byte mutation cannot stumble into. [FAIR-SCOPE: localizer supplies call structure]

ground_truth.placement: in CommandDispatcher, success branch after a command executes (Exit: / TPM_RC_SUCCESS)

llm_proposal.why_stuck: The fuzzer cannot progress past the parameter unmarshaling loop because any failure returns the same error code, producing no new edge coverage regardless of how many parameters were successfully unmarshaled. Without feedback on partial successes, mutation cannot build up the correct sequence of parameter fields.
llm_proposal.relevant_state: pNum, the counter of parameters processed in the unmarshaling loop
llm_proposal.placement_reason: Inside the parameter unmarshaling loop, after pNum is incremented so that each iteration's progress count is exposed, providing a gradient toward successfully unmarshaling more parameters.
